title: PayPal Disputes
bodyHtml:
PayPal Disputes

This article aims at providing you guidelines to follow in case a customer creates a dispute on PayPal. For general information on this payment method, you may discover more insights about how does Paypal work on Back Market in the recommended article.

Please note that these guidelines aim at helping you be proactive, however we cannot guarantee that PayPal will solve the dispute in your favor by providing the information mentioned here.


Overview on PayPal Disputes

When do customers open disputes?
Customers purchasing through PayPal have the right to dispute the transaction in case an issue occurs, see the PayPal guidelines on this topic.

For your information, generally on our platform customers tend to dispute the delivery of the product or that the device purchased does not match the description of the listing. Customers may also open a dispute if the refund of the order fails (to avoid this from happening, see the paragraph “Known issues, 2)” in our article PayPal: How does it work on Back Market? 


What is the role of Back Market when a customer disputes a transaction? 

Please note that Back Market is not able to influence PayPal's decisions in any way. Opening a dispute on a transaction is a solution customers may opt for if they experience an issue. We are not able to contact PayPal to provide advice though, as PayPal operates independently. 

In case of a PayPal dispute, please make sure you provide as much evidence as possible within the timeframe given by PayPal, in order to build a case in your favor.
 

What evidence should I provide to PayPal?

PayPal allows you to challenge what the customer declares within a specific timeframe, as mentioned in their guidelines. 

As soon as you are notified (generally on PayPal) that a dispute has been opened on a given transaction, reach out to PayPal directly and provide as much information as possible to prove that you have acted correctly. 

Some evidence that you should think of providing may be:

  • Extract of the conversation with the customer in cases of disputes on defective products
  • Details of shipments or links to the shipping provider's website in cases of disputes on non-received parcels
  • Any other evidence you may think of (screenshots from the Back Office to prove that the order exists on Back Market, etc.)


Use Case

For example, a customer ordered an iPhone 11 and has opened a PayPal dispute to claim that they never received the parcel. You, on your side, have all the elements to prove that you acted as expected. You can reach out to PayPal as soon as you are notified of the dispute creation and provide:

  • The tracking link showing that the parcel was sent from your warehouse to the customer’s address
  • The weight of the parcel, to show that it matches the expected weight of this kind of product
  • Proof of delivery with the signature of the customer
  • Screenshots from your database, showing that the device with this IMEI was shipped to the customer
  • Screenshots from our Back Office, showing that the order was placed through Back Market, or the conversation with the customer
  • ...

Such elements can really help you build the case in your favour and increase the possibility to win the dispute. 
 

What is the role of the SSC?

While we are always available to provide further insights or advice on specific cases, bear in mind that you need to seek any further assistance from PayPal directly in case you do not agree with the outcome of a specific case. As specified in our T&Cs, you need to rely on the Seller Protection Policy of PayPal.
